title: AI Automation Engineer, Associate Level
description:
Job Type: Hybrid (2-3 days a week onsite)

MUST BE A LOCAL CANDIDATE

** NO Visa Sponsorships or Transfers Available (including OPT) **

Job Overview

Devonshire Recruiting & Consulting Partners is seeking to identify an Associate-level AI Automation Engineer to support the development of intelligent automation capabilities across the organization of a leading tech company. This newly created position is well-suited for an early-career technologist with strong foundational skills and a clear understanding of how modern AI tools can be applied to real-world business workflows.

The role offers exposure to emerging AI frameworks, automation platforms, and agent-based architectures, working closely with consultants and internal stakeholders to design, prototype, and deploy scalable automation solutions that enhance operational efficiency and innovation.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design and implement AI-enabled automation workflows and intelligent agents to streamline business processes.
  • Integrate large language models and AI services (commercial and open-source) with enterprise systems, including APIs, databases, CRMs, and collaboration tools.
  • Support rapid prototyping efforts and assist in transitioning proofs of concept into production-ready solutions.
  • Test, debug, and optimize automation scripts and AI agents for reliability and performance.
  • Monitor developments in AI tooling, frameworks, and platforms to identify opportunities for innovation.
  • Create and maintain technical documentation to support knowledge sharing and long-term maintainability.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or a related technical discipline.
  • 1–2 years of relevant professional experience in automation, software development, or applied AI.
  • Proficient in Python, including experience with scripting, APIs, or workflow automation.
  • Working knowledge of AI concepts such as large language models, prompt design, and natural language processing.
  • Familiarity with agent-based or orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain, LangGraph, Pydantic AI, CrewAI, or similar).
  • Exposure to retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) patterns and AI tool integration concepts.
  • Experience with workflow or ETL tools, either open-source (e.g., Airflow, Prefect, DBT) or platform-based (e.g., Databricks, GCP Workflows, Azure Data Factory).
  • Basic understanding of cloud environments (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and version control systems such as Git.
  • Strong analytical mindset, curiosity, and ability to learn new technologies quickly.
  • Solid communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across technical and non-technical teams.

title: AI Data Engineer
description:
Devonshire Recruitment & Consulting Partners is actively seeking an AI Data Engineer to join a specialized team dedicated to leveraging data science and AI to drive strategic business solutions. This role involves translating business challenges into structured analytical approaches, refining large datasets, and developing advanced AI models to support decision-making.

Key Responsibilities
  • Collaborate with internal teams and clients to understand business objectives, key metrics, and analytical needs.
  • Develop structured analytical frameworks to address complex business questions through data-driven insights.
  • Extract, transform, and process large-scale datasets to support statistical modeling and machine learning applications.
  • Identify and resolve data integrity issues while documenting findings and resolutions.
  • Design, implement, and optimize machine learning and artificial intelligence models, including generative AI solutions.
  • Establish validation processes to ensure the accuracy and reliability of AI-driven models before deployment.
  • Develop intuitive dashboards and visual reports to communicate insights effectively to stakeholders.
  • Present analytical findings in a clear and actionable manner, translating complex data into business recommendations.
Required Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or equivalent experience.
  • Prior experience in a data-driven consulting role.
  • 3-4 years of experience in SQL or Python for data analysis and transformation, automating workflows using Python, SQL, or Bash scripting.
  • Hands-on experience with data preparation for machine learning and statistical modeling.
  • Familiarity with cloud computing platforms such as AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Snowflake, and Databricks.
  • Understanding of ETL processes and experience with tools like Apache Airflow, AWS Glue, DBT, or Alteryx.
  • Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work with stakeholders to refine analytical needs.
  • Ability to translate data findings into business insights with strong attention to detail.
  • Proven experience in managing projects and delivering results within tight deadlines.
Desired Skills and Qualifications
  • Master’s Degree in Data Science, Business Analytics, or related field.
  • Experience managing ETL processes within MySQL, PostgreSQL, MSSQL, or Oracle SQL.
  • Proficiency in Apache Spark and distributed computing frameworks.
  • Knowledge of BI tools such as Tableau, Power BI, Looker, or Qlik.
  • Experience working with geospatial data and analytics.

title: Assistant Controller
description:
** IMPORTANT: Must have strong knowledge of US GAAP, IFRS, and FERC Uniform System of Accounts and hold an active CPA accreditation **

Devonshire Recruiting & Consulting Partners is looking for an experienced Assistant Controller to support financial reporting and controls for New York-based operating companies. This role will work closely with the Vice President of Controllership and Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A), ensuring the accuracy of financial statements, compliance with regulatory reporting requirements, and the implementation of robust internal controls. The ideal candidate will have the ability to navigate complex financial matters, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and influence key business decisions.

Key Responsibilities
  • Oversee the preparation and validation of financial statements, ensuring compliance with US GAAP, IFRS, FERC, and other regulatory requirements.
  • Manage full financial reporting for New York operating entities, including Profit & Loss (P&L) statements, balance sheets, and key accounting estimates.
  • Serve as the primary point of contact for external auditors, leading annual audits and interim reviews.
  • Drive the implementation of a strong internal controls framework and ensure adherence to compliance requirements.
  • Review and approve technical accounting memos, impairment assessments, and key financial reporting disclosures.
  • Lead interactions with the Enterprise Business Services team to ensure timely and accurate reporting of financial data.
  • Work closely with the FP&A team to generate financial forecasts and assess budget variances.
  • Support regulatory reporting and rate case proceedings by providing financial expertise and insights.
  • Partner with Treasury on capital market transactions, including debt issuances and financial structuring.
  • Identify and mitigate financial risks, working proactively with the risk and compliance teams.
Required Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. CPA or Chartered Accountant certification is required.
  • 10+ years of experience in financial reporting, accounting, and controllership roles, ideally within a regulated industry.
  • Must have strong knowledge of US GAAP, IFRS, and FERC Uniform System of Accounts.
  • Expertise in regulatory accounting (ASC 980) and compliance standards, including SOX.
  • Proven ability to manage financial close processes, external audits, and financial disclosures.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to translate complex financial data into actionable insights.
  • Ability to build strong relationships and collaborate across multiple teams, including finance, treasury, risk, and regulatory affairs.
  • Experience managing teams and working within matrix organizational structures.
  • Excellent communication skills with the ability to present financial data to senior leadership.

title: Business Process Analyst
description:
Job Title: Business Process Analyst
Job Type: CONTRACT (4 months – potential extension)
Location: Flexible On-site/Hybrid/Remote (EST or CST)

** No H1B, OPT or C2C **

Job Overview:

Devonshire Recruitment & Consulting Partners is seeking an experienced Business Process Analyst to support a Life Sciences client with documenting current-state workflows, defining future-state processes, and mapping how work moves across teams and enterprise systems—particularly the ERP environment. This role requires strong process modeling skills, excellent stakeholder communication, and the ability to understand how operational processes connect across Finance, Operations, Program Management, Data, and IT.

Key Responsibilities
  • Partner with business and technical stakeholders to capture end-to-end workflows.
  • Document current-state and future-state processes, system interactions, and data flows.
  • Analyze dependencies between business processes and core systems, including the ERP.
  • Identify inefficiencies, integration gaps, and improvement opportunities.
  • Create standardized process maps and documentation using Visio or similar tools.
  • Validate findings with SMEs and support ERP optimization and transformation efforts.
  • Prepare clear summaries and presentations for program leadership.
Required Qualifications
  • 5+ years in business/process analysis; 2+ years in Life Sciences.
  • Strong background in mapping cross-functional workflows tied to ERP or enterprise systems.
  • Experience documenting complex processes across Finance, Operations, and related teams.
  • Proficiency with process mapping tools (Visio preferred).
  • Excellent communication, analytical, and facilitation skills.
Desirable Skills
  • Familiarity with Lean/Six Sigma methodologies.
  • Hands-on exposure to ERP platforms (D365, SAP, Oracle).
  • Understanding of data governance and multi-system integration.

Key Outputs
  • Complete current-state and future-state process documentation.
  • System/data flow diagrams centered on ERP interactions.
  • Gap analysis with recommendations for improvement.
  • Executive-ready summary of findings.

title: Vice President, Chief Accounting Officer (CAO)
description:
Job Overview

Devonshire Recruiting & Consulting Partners is seeking a Vice President, Chief Accounting Officer (CAO). Reporting to the CFO, the CAO will lead global accounting operations, ensure financial reporting integrity, and drive operational excellence as a key member of the finance leadership team. This high-visibility role is perfect for a strategic, collaborative leader with deep expertise in U.S. GAAP, SEC reporting, and SOX compliance, and a passion for building scalable, high-performing teams in a fast-paced, growth-oriented environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Oversee global accounting functions, including general ledger, technical accounting, SEC reporting, SOX compliance, and accounts payable.
  • Deliver accurate, timely, and compliant financial reports (10-Ks, 10-Qs, earnings releases) in line with U.S. GAAP and SEC regulations.
  • Design and implement efficient processes, standard operating procedures, and internal controls to enhance accuracy and scalability.
  • Serve as the primary point of contact for external auditors, regulators, and stakeholders on financial reporting and accounting policies.
  • Provide strategic guidance on complex accounting issues, including revenue recognition, R&D, M&A, and equity instruments.
  • Lead process optimization and automation initiatives using lean methodologies to support operational excellence.
  • Mentor and develop a high-performing accounting team, fostering collaboration, professional growth, and accountability.
  • Partner with FP&A, Legal, Tax, Treasury, and business units to align financial reporting with organizational goals.
  • Monitor and assess emerging accounting standards and regulatory changes to ensure compliance and strategic alignment.

Team Leadership

  • Empower the accounting team by clarifying roles, enhancing skills, and implementing training programs to achieve individual and organizational excellence.
  • Foster a collaborative, inclusive team culture that promotes integrity, innovation, and professional development.
  • Drive automation and process improvements to streamline workflows and accelerate delivery timelines.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field; CPA required.
  • 15+ years of progressive accounting experience, including 5+ years in a senior leadership role at a publicly traded company.
  • Deep expertise in U.S. GAAP, SEC reporting, and SOX compliance; experience in the life sciences, biotech, or pharmaceutical industries is a strong plus.
  • Proven success leading teams through complex transactions, such as mergers and acquisitions (M&A) and integrations, is preferred.
  • Exceptional leadership and communication skills, with the ability to engage with executive leadership, Audit Committees, and external stakeholders.
  • Strategic, hands-on mindset with a track record of driving process improvements and scalable team structures.
  • Strong interpersonal skills, adept at translating complex financial concepts for finance and non-finance audiences.
  • Commitment to integrity, credibility, and fostering an inclusive, high-performance team environment.

title: D365 Functional Analyst
description:
Job Overview

Devonshire Recruiting & Consulting Partners is actively seeking a D365 Functional Analyst to support our client’s Finance systems by designing, configuring, and optimizing Microsoft Dynamics 365. This role will collaborate closely with internal Finance teams and IT stakeholders to gather requirements, build tailored solutions, and ensure efficient system performance through continual enhancement and support.

This is a hands-on functional role ideal for candidates with experience translating complex business needs into robust D365 configurations, supporting user adoption, and driving ongoing platform improvements.

Key Responsibilities
  • Partner with Finance stakeholders to gather functional requirements and document business processes.
  • Analyze system gaps and recommend scalable solutions that align with Dynamics 365 functionality.
  • Configure D365 modules, workflows, data structures, and business rules to meet user and business needs.
  • Lead unit testing and support user acceptance testing (UAT); validate solution accuracy and system behavior.
  • Deliver user training and develop documentation to promote effective system use.
  • Monitor system performance, identify areas for optimization, and propose process automation opportunities.
  • Stay current on Microsoft D365 updates, patches, and emerging best practices.
  • Collaborate with IT, project managers, and business users to support system upgrades, integrations, and enhancements.
  • Contribute to change management by communicating key system changes and updates.
  • Maintain confidentiality of financial data and adhere to company policies and procedures.
  • Support additional functional or technical duties as assigned.
Required Qualifications
  • 2–5 years of experience in a Functional Analyst role supporting Microsoft Dynamics 365 (Finance modules).
  • Familiarity with financial business processes; experience working directly with finance and accounting teams.
  • Strong understanding of D365 system architecture, configuration tools, and workflow management.
  • Microsoft D365 certifications preferred.
  • Proficient in Agile project environments and requirement-gathering techniques.
  • Excellent problem-solving capabilities and structured thinking.
  • Strong communication skills, both written and verbal.
  • Organized and detail-oriented with a proactive mindset.

title: Director of DevOps
description:
Devonshire Recruiting and Consulting Partners is currently seeking a Director of DevOps for our local San Diego-based client.  The ideal candidate will have experience developing a DevOps process and collaborate with our client’s development, production and operations teams.  You will also be responsible for building out cloud initiatives, deployment needs and supporting their SQL Server enterprise environment.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science or equivalent
  • 4+ years of experience working and leading in DevOps role
  • Experience building out development operations in previous enterprise environments
  • Strong process and methodology development experience and understanding Lean Engineering, Agile, Scrum and DevOps
  • Experience with multiple platforms including Windows, Linux, Azure and/or AWS
  • Strong back end data knowledge using SQL Server, DocumentDB, MongoDB
  • Development environment knowledge using C#, Node.JS, PowerShell, JavaScript, Java, Python and/or Ruby
  • Frameworks: .NET, ASP.Net MVC, AngularJS, Bootstrap, J2EE, Spring, Struts, Flow, Log4Net
  • Tools knowledge includes: Visual Studio, Team Foundation Server, Release Manager, MSBuild, Git

title: Director of FP&A & BI
description:
Devonshire Recruiting & Consulting Partners is seeking a talented Director of FP&A & BI to lead a team of analysts dedicated to annual planning. Forecasting business performance and providing valuable insights that help leaders across America make informed strategic decisions. This role is critical to enhancing the technical capabilities of the team. Creating a strong reporting structure, ensuring budget alignment with business goals, and driving growth. The director will also serve as a key partner providing in-depth financial analysis and business intelligence across channels such as wholesale, retail, and e-commerce.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Provide strategic insights and guidance to the executive team by guiding and supporting business planning, analysis, and short- and long-term growth initiatives.
  • Develop tools for business users to increase access to self-service financial information. and organize training to promote wider adoption.
  • Lead key financial forecasting processes. Includes annual financial planning/budget (AFP), strategic business planning (SBP), and year-end forecast (YEP).
  • Provide regular financial analysis, including monthly and quarterly business reviews. Mid-month forecast and participation in committee presentations and business evaluations.
  • Work with critical business functions across wholesale, retail, e-commerce, and operations. To provide insights and financial assistance
  • Leading the way in developing and improving business intelligence frameworks. To ensure data-driven decision-making and transparency across the organization.
  • Oversee business analysis projects Work with the management team to explore alternative business models and sustainable growth.
  • Manage high-quality forecasting processes Ensure business forecasts for both short-term and long-term initiatives.
  • Identify risks and opportunities through business intelligence and financial analysis. To ensure alignment with broader business objectives
  • Prepare financial audits regarding sales volumes, prices, cost analysis. and operating expenses
  • Evaluate future investments and growth opportunities to support the company’s long-term goals.
  • Work with the finance and accounting team during month-end and year-end financial closes. They provide key reports and insights through local team leaders.
  • Deliver numerous reports and presentations throughout the year. Including business reviews Budget reviews and forecasts and specialized financial analysis
  • Advise the executive team and board on appropriate strategies to position the company for future growth.
  • Lead cross-functional project teams to create business plans and strategic models.
  • Analyze financial data and other operational indicators. Summary of key findings and provide actionable advice to senior management.
  • Explore resources Analysis tools and new methods continuously to improve business efficiency
  • Manage and develop team members effectively. Provide training and opportunities for professional growth.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or related field.
  • MBA (highly preferred) or equivalent experience in a business environment using analytical techniques.
  • At least 10 years of experience in financial planning, analysis, or related field.
  • At least 5 years of experience leading enterprise-wide budgeting or strategy projects in organizations with revenues over $200 million.
  • Experience of 3+ years in people management.
  • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office with a strong ability to translate complex financial data into presentations using PowerPoint.
  • Proficient in SQL programming language will be given special consideration.
  • Those with SAP experience will be given special consideration.
  • Excellent oral and written English communication skills.
  • Willingness to travel domestically and abroad up to 10%.

title: Field Sales Engineer
description:
Hiring a Field Sales Engineer for our North TX Sales territory. This person will be responsible for selling our entire product line (HPLC, GC, MS, Spectroscopy, UV-Vis, TOC, etc.). This position affords the ability to have an excellent base salary, uncapped commissions, and a company vehicle! Details are below:

Position Summary:
The individual in this position is responsible for a wide variety of sales related functions including sales of all assigned product lines, demonstrations, installations, training, reports as required, the maintenance and safekeeping of demonstration instrumentation, computers, software, company vehicles and other company property, as well as, other related duties which may be assigned from time to time.

This position will be required to reach or exceed assigned sales goals within an assigned geographic territory.

title: iOS Mobile Developer
description:
Position Summary
Devonshire is seeking a senior level Objective-C/iOS software engineer to help develop and enhance a mobile application that is slated to be a industry disruptor.  You will be part of a fun, fast-paced and highly collaborative product development team leveraging Agile Scrum to deliver new functionality in incremental iterations.  You will work closely with product management and our customers to understand and refine market requirements. You will be hands-on, implementing alongside other developers while championing a clean, pragmatic design for the entire system.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities

  • Design, develop, code, and test advanced level iOS software apps to support achievement of product roadmaps and overall business objectives.
  • Determine operating feasibility by evaluating analysis, problem definition, requirements, solution development and proposed solutions, and provide alternative solutions as required.
  • Partner collaboratively with the business and project team to accomplish tasks/milestones/goals.
  • Document and demonstrate solutions by developing documentation, flowcharts, layouts, diagrams, charts, and code comments.
  • Improve operations by conducting systems analysis; recommending changes in policy and procedures.
  • Provide estimates of work effort and impact of projects and tasks, and provide team leadership, as required.
  • Update and distribute job knowledge by studying state-of-the-art development tools, programming techniques, computing equipment and by following Apple technology updates.

 

Education, Experience, Certification Requirements

  • Bachelor’s Degree in computer science or related field, or its equivalent.
  • 5 years of related experience in full cycle software development with demonstrated achievements and progressive responsibilities.
  • 2-5 years of iOS experience

Knowledge, Skills and Abilities

  • Highly proficient and demonstrated expert and consultative level technical software design, debugging, documentation, and testing skills, at the subject matter expert level necessary to complete and document projects
  • Comprehensive knowledge of professional software development process and concepts, with the ability to lead and teach others.
  • Advanced level proficiency and breadth in required code development in area of specialization.
  • Developed and demonstrated proficiency to resolve a wide range of moderately complex business problems and opportunities where analysis of data requires evaluation of identifiable factors.
  • Demonstrated proficiency and application of business software lifecycle development.
  • Excellent and demonstrated communication skills (verbal, written, wiki and white-boarding).
  • Comprehensive knowledge of Agile methodology.
  • Ability to communicate and coordinate projects in a team leadership capacity.
  • Knowledge of Objective-C, iOS, XCode and iOS/3rd party libraries
  • Experience integrating mobile apps to server side systems using RESTful APIs and/or SOAP
  • Familiarity and understanding of Apple’s Human Interface Guidelines so as to guide/advice the UX/UI team

title: Lead Mechanical Engineer (Materials)
description:
Job Overview

Devonshire Recruiting & Consulting Partners is partnering with a major energy provider to hire a Lead Mechanical Engineer (Materials). This senior role is central to maintaining the safety, reliability, and modernization of a large generation fleet and supporting new energy projects. You will provide technical leadership in mechanical integrity, failure analysis, and capital project execution within a multidisciplinary engineering team.

This engineering position will support both internal and external teams working on new energy projects and improvements to our existing fleet of steam and combustion turbine units. This role involves inspecting and evaluating power boilers, pressure vessels, process tanks, high-energy piping systems, and other mechanical components.


The engineer will assess and implement upgrades as part of capital improvement and maintenance programs. Responsibilities include mechanical design evaluations, material analysis, cost/benefit and risk assessments, and performance reviews. The role also includes project engineering, design, and management duties, along with the development of mechanical specifications, standards, and procedures.

This position is a key contributor to our asset management and technology modernization efforts.

Key Responsibilities

  • Conduct mechanical integrity inspections and failure analysis for boilers, pressure vessels, high-energy piping, and related systems.
  • Lead condition assessments and develop repair strategies for critical power generation assets.
  • Serve as Project Engineer/Manager for capital upgrade projects from scope development through commissioning.
  • Perform engineering calculations and technical evaluations in compliance with ASME, National Board, and API codes.
  • Develop mechanical specifications, standards, and maintenance procedures.
  • Provide field troubleshooting support and verify as-built conditions.
  • Prepare cost estimates and perform risk/cost-benefit analyses for engineering solutions.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical or Materials Engineering.
  • Active New York State Professional Engineer (PE) license.
  • Minimum of 10 years of experience in power generation or related heavy industry.
  • Expertise in ASME Sections I, II, V, VIII, IX, B31.1, National Board codes, and API 650/653.
  • Proven experience in materials evaluation, failure analysis, and welding technology.
  • Ability to work on-site in a power plant environment and travel locally as needed.
  • Strong leadership, communication, and project management skills.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite.

title: .NET Web Developer
description:
Devonshire Recruiting and Consulting Partners is in search of a dynamic . with comprehensive client-side focus and strong back-end coding knowledge as well. Our client’s environment is mostly Microsoft: ASP.NET, VB.NET, MVC, C# and SQL Server 2012.


Responsibilities:

  • Must have understanding of n-tier architecture and OOPS concepts
  • Understand master page, content page, user controls and passing/retrieving values from SQL Server database
  • Business layer, reporting and full stack web browser development in ASP.NET, C#, VB.NET
  • Must be able to write complex stored procedures, queries, functions, views, etc.
  • Should know temp tables/variables and come with broad understanding of cursors
  • Design, develop and enhance back-end and front-end browser components
  • Understand responsive designs patter for all device/browsers (IE, Chrome, Firefox, Safari/Mobile, desktop screens)
  • Sitecore or any such CMS integration is a plus


Qualifications:

  • 3+ years of development experience with ASP.NET and C# web layer code,
  • Strong experience working with ASP.NET, C#, SQL, JavaScript/jQuery, MVC
  • Agile development experience is preferred
  • Internal business applications experience is preferred
  • Excellent communication skills, ability to work well in a team setting and work independently

title: Principal Application Security Engineer
description:
Job Overview

Devonshire Recruitment & Consulting Partners is actively seeking a Principal Application Security Engineer to provide strategic security guidance across a diverse product portfolio. This high-impact role focuses on shaping secure design practices, influencing product security strategies, and partnering with development teams to deliver secure solutions from inception to release.

Key Responsibilities

  • Advise development teams on secure architecture, risk trade-offs, and best practices.
  • Facilitate threat modeling and architecture review sessions.
  • Establish and promote security standards, frameworks, and reusable blueprints.
  • Consult with stakeholders to align security recommendations with business goals.
  • Influence application security program initiatives and awareness efforts.

Required Qualifications

  • 6+ years in security consulting, architecture, or advisory roles with strong application security experience (SAST, DAST, SCA, etc.).
  • Proven expertise in security assessments, threat modeling, and secure code reviews.
  • Strong knowledge of modern development practices, cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP), and containerization technologies (Docker, Kubernetes).
  • Proficiency with security testing tools such as Synopsys, CodeQL, CSPM, etc.
  • Solid understanding of enterprise security principles and frameworks.

Desired Skills & Qualifications

  • Security certifications (CISSP, SABSA, TOGAF) preferred.
  • Familiarity with regulated industries such as healthcare.
  • Experience developing security programs or frameworks.
  • Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills.

title: Principal Data Architect / Team Lead
description:
Devonshire Recruiting and Consulting is currently seeking a Principal Data Architect to lead our client’s Analytics team. This position will have analytics development, product development and practice management responsibilities.

Analytics Development and Management Responsibilities:

· Lead and manage the team by setting career goals, quarterly objectives, and delivering ongoing technical, business, and project management feedback and coaching

· Support consulting teams to rapidly develop advanced analytics that help clients transform and improve their businesses

· Lead development, upgrades, and maintenance of proprietary databases that support consulting work

· Evaluate tools and technologies for use by the team and consulting teams to improve ability to manipulate and analyze large datasets

· Lead rapid prototyping of analytics into deliverables in a project

· Partner with Directors and Principals to explain the depth and uniqueness of their capabilities to customers and prospects

· Assist with scoping and pricing of projects for clients
Product Development Responsibilities:

· Lead and manage all aspects of the two product platforms based on client demand. Formulate and define objectives, goals and execution plan

· Collect input from Directors and Principals to determine key product evolution and features

· Blend business and technical expertise to translate client and product requirements to technical architecture and requirements. Oversee the engineering team and work closely with other functions within the company to deliver timely and robust solutions

· Build the processes and tools, and organization necessary to sell, develop features, customize, and support each of the products

· Understand the design and architecture of the existing software platforms, and contribute to the implementation of new features and technical improvements

· Assist in project scoping by estimating delivery effort based on feature complexity and team velocity

· Manage multiple releases and release cycles across multiple customers

· Interact with client’s technical teams to explain software architecture, review technology stack, and conduct code reviews
Practice Management Responsibilities:

· Manage P&L for Advanced Analytics Team and product-level P&Ls for the two product platforms.

· Determine resources needs and investment

· Work with CFO to determine pricing strategy for three software tools accounting for value creation, competition, and internal costs

· Manage a geographically-distributed team comprised of employees and independent contractors. Source contractors or contract engineering firms when needed

· Recruit and develop engineering talent

· Define and track metrics to measure AIT goals over time, communicate on an ongoing basis with

· Directors and Principals on goals, objectives, plans and actions being undertaken

· Provide ongoing, real-time feedback to the team and draft regular performance reviews. Partner with AIT members on professional development opportunities

· Work with CFO, HR and advisory team to manage individuals’ performance and adjust compensation accordingly

· Work with staffing and client teams to allocate resources to consulting projects where needed

· Interface with IT team to determine technology requirements and investments to support existing platforms and ad hoc solutions. Identify trends and anticipate upcoming needs/investments

Requirements:

· 10+ years of experience leading and managing software development

· Extensive experience in leading teams building Enterprise grade user-facing, web-based applications

· Extensive experience with AWS

· Extensive experience with big data processing (Hadoop/spark) and analytics

· Experience with various databases (e.g. Postgres, MySQL, PostGIS, Redshift or other OLAP DB, Mongodb, etc)

· Expert knowledge of algorithm design/implementation, mathematics, and statistics

· Expert ability to manage geographically distributed engineering teams and communicate with clients and senior staff regarding product specifications

· Expert ability to manage outsourcing and contract partners

· Superior communication skills to share product capabilities, positioning and vision internally and with clients

· Expert knowledge in data structures, database design, source code management, and Agile development methodologies

· Application of relevant skills to solve complex business problems, and management of work under tight time constraints

· General management experience

· Experience in management consulting, prior experience in marketing or telecom network design a plus

· Advanced degree in computer science, mathematics, or engineering or equivalent work experience

For more information, please apply online.

title: Principal of Analytics and Strategy
location: (Hybrid) Greater Boston, New York City, or San Francisco
description:
*** NO VISA SPONSORSHIPS OR TRANSFERS ***

Job Overview

Devonshire Recruitment & Consulting Partners is actively seeking an experienced Principal of Analytics and Strategy to join a globally recognized strategy consultancy operating exclusively within the advisory space. This is a senior leadership opportunity for someone who thrives at the intersection of technical delivery and business strategy. The right candidate will take full ownership of data-driven engagements from inception to completion while also contributing meaningfully to the firm’s broader corporate strategy work on both transactional and long-term mandates.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design original analytical frameworks that translate complex client challenges into clear, actionable components and recommendations
  • Lead junior team members while serving as the primary client-facing point of contact throughout the engagement lifecycle
  • Define technical architecture and deliverable scope for large-scale mandates while managing stakeholder alignment at every stage
  • Identify and close organizational capability gaps using internal resources, partnerships, or external hires
  • Adapt to shifting goals, deadlines, and technical complexities as engagements evolve
  • Oversee functional and technical governance across the full delivery lifecycle
  • Leverage existing tools and platforms to capture new capabilities back into the firm’s knowledge base
  • Contribute to business development and the global analytics practice growth through recruiting and best practice development
  • Integration of AI tools and agents into new data sets

Required Qualifications

  • 8+ years of professional experience with a minimum of 5 years in a consulting or strategy consulting environment
  • Master’s degree in a relevant field
  • Proven solution architect with hands-on exposure to Python, SQL, Tableau, ArcGIS/QGIS, and cloud-based ML platforms such as Databricks
  • Familiarity with enterprise architecture frameworks and experience operating within global, distributed team environments

title: Sales Compensation and Commissions Analyst
description:
Devonshire Recruitment & Consulting Partners is actively seeking a Sales Compensation and Commissions Analyst to join a dynamic Sales Operations team. This full-time, on-site role is an exciting opportunity for professionals with a strong background in sales commission analysis and data analytics.

Key Responsibilities

  • Collaborate with sales management, operations, and the Customer Fulfillment Center to review and analyze data, ensuring accurate sales transaction credits for representatives.
  • Oversee monthly and quarterly changes to sales compensation plans.
  • Manage the Deal Registration and Commission Deviation processes.
  • Coordinate with cross-functional teams to investigate, address, and resolve compensation disputes, implementing improved processes to prevent discrepancies.
  • Handle issue escalations and respond to inquiries from sales teams, ensuring timely resolution of errors or concerns.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s Degree is required.
  • 2-5 years of experience in compensation and sales commission analysis.
  • Proficiency in advanced Microsoft Excel, including reports, dashboards, pivot tables, and lookups.
  • Experience with Salesforce is a plus.

title: Senior Cloud Security Engineer
description:
Job Overview

Devonshire Recruitment & Consulting Partners seeks a Senior Cloud Security Engineer to strengthen and optimize multi-cloud security across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud environments. This role focuses on leading Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) initiatives, establishing secure cloud baselines, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to ensure a strong, scalable, and compliant security posture.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead the implementation and optimization of CSPM solutions across multiple cloud platforms.
  • Define and maintain secure baseline configurations for AWS, Azure, and GCP.
  • Establish continuous compliance monitoring and real-time alerting for security issues.
  • Develop cloud security guidance, best practices, and architecture patterns.
  • Collaborate with engineering and DevOps teams to integrate security controls into cloud deployments.

Required Qualifications

  • 5+ years in information security with at least 3 years in cloud security.
  • Hands-on experience with at least two major cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP).
  • Proven track record in implementing and managing CSPM solutions.
  • Strong understanding of cloud-native security services and compliance frameworks (CIS, NIST, ISO, etc.).
  • Proficiency in scripting and automation tools such as Python, PowerShell, or Bash.

Desired Skills & Qualifications

  • Security certifications such as CCSP, AWS Security Specialty, Azure Security Engineer, or GCP Professional Cloud Security Engineer.
  • Knowledge of container security, DevSecOps practices, and Zero Trust architectures.
  • Experience with identity and access management across cloud platforms.

title: Senior Consultant (Data & Analytics)
description:
Job Overview

Devonshire Recruiting & Consulting Partners is seeking a Senior Consultant to join an Analytics Innovation team in NYC. This hybrid role provides an exciting opportunity for experienced professionals to lead workstreams and oversee teams solving complex analytical challenges for clients in the telecommunications and technology sectors.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead and manage entire workstreams, overseeing Data Analysts and Data Engineers in solving complex analytical problems.
  • Lead client meetings, foster relationships, and formulate hypotheses to address strategic questions.
  • Oversee data preparation, data model building, and results visualization to connect insights with client objectives.
  • Build and maintain master databases/data warehouses by consolidating disparate data sources.
  • Conduct quality audits to identify and address potential data quality issues while overseeing teamwork products.
  • Create and present slide decks that effectively communicate complex analytical concepts to stakeholders.

Required Qualifications

  • Master’s degree in Data Science or a related field plus one (1) year of relevant experience; OR Bachelor’s degree in Data Science or a related field plus three (3) years of relevant experience.
  • Expertise in leading data projects
  • Advanced experience working with SQL, R and/or Python for complex modeling and analyses.
  • Strong proficiency in preparing data for statistical or machine learning models.
  • Proven ability to run ETLs on data warehouses, including AWS Redshift, Presto, Hive, Big Query, or Teradata, as well as traditional relational databases (MSQL, PostgreSQL, MSSQL, Oracle SQL), and using Apache Spark for big data processing.
  • Familiarity with cloud platforms like GCP, AWS, or similar systems.
  • Experience exporting data to Excel and publishing results to BI tools such as Tableau, Qlik, Looker, or Power BI.
  • Proficiency with any flavor of GIS for geospatial analysis.
  • Demonstrated ability to translate data into actionable insights and present them effectively.

title: Senior Director of FP&A
description:
Devonshire Recruiting & Consulting Partners is looking for a Director of Financial Planning & Analysis. This critical leadership role will be required to report directly to the Chief Financial Officer. The ideal fit will be responsible for leading the financial planning and reporting functions of the field operations team, employing advanced financial management techniques tailored to the senior living industry. Additionally, will play a pivotal role in leveraging information technology to support and monitor the execution of senior living operations strategies.

Key Responsibilities:

  1. Financial Leadership & Strategy:
    • Lead the financial planning and analysis function, including budgeting, forecasting, and long-term planning to support strategic initiatives and business decision-making.
    • Act as a key finance business partner to the leadership team, collaborating closely with the VP of Operations to ensure financial alignment with business objectives.
    • Conduct high-impact analyses of business results, identifying trends, variances, and potential opportunities, and provide strategic recommendations to senior leadership for business growth and operational efficiency.
  2. Training & Development:
    • Develop and deliver a training curriculum for new community and regional leaders to enhance their financial acumen, covering budgeting/forecasting, SOPs, and other critical financial metrics.
  3. Team Leadership & Development:
    • Lead, develop, and oversee a team of analysts and managers, providing strategic financial support for regional and community teams across Operations, Sales, Clinical, Maintenance, and Dining departments.
    • Establish clear goals and expectations, provide constructive feedback, and encourage open dialogue to help team members reach their full potential.
  4. Financial Analysis & Reporting:
    • Oversee comprehensive financial analysis and modeling to support business decisions, and identify cost optimization, revenue enhancement, and margin improvement opportunities through scenario modeling.
    • Establish and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure financial and operational performance, and implement best practices to improve financial efficiency and profitability.
    • Manage the budgeting and forecasting process, collaborate across the organization to support critical strategic decision-making, and provide financial transparency to operations leadership through real-time analysis.
  5. Technology & Process Improvement:
    • Assist operations and liaise with Information Technology on the buildout of business intelligence data sets for easy real-time analysis of senior living operations.
    • Continuously improve FP&A processes, tools, and methodologies to enhance the efficiency, accuracy, and value-add of the function.
    • Ensure the organization has the right reporting capabilities to deliver real-time KPI measurement.

Supervisory Responsibilities:

  • Directly supervise the FP&A team, hiring and retaining talent aligned with organizational goals and mission.
  • Build strong, collaborative teams and lead by example, setting clear goals and expectations, and fostering a culture of open communication and continuous development.

Requirements:

  • Education: Bachelor’s degree required, MBA preferred. Equivalent combination of education and experience is considered.
  • Experience: 10 to 15 years of related experience in financial management, with a strong preference for experience in multi-unit senior living operations.
  • Skills & Competencies:
    • Strong financial management and business acumen.
    • Exceptional communication proficiency and ethical conduct.
    • Proven leadership, performance management, and strategic thinking abilities.
    • Proficient problem-solving and analysis skills.
    • Familiarity with core financial and operations systems such as Workday, DOMO, OnShift, Salesforce, and PointClickCare.
    • Highly proficient with Excel.

title: Senior Finance Business Partner – Burlington, MA
description:
The Finance Business Partner will have an important role in providing decision support to the business units by providing strategic insights, analysis, and reporting around financial priorities. Support will include a focus on engagement (managed) views of financials as well as understanding profitability drivers and trends.

This role is responsible for providing support to leadership to ensure strong understanding of business operations and performance – through an engagement (managed) lens. This role will interface with a variety of business leaders including VPs and Directors, Lead Finance Business Partners, Senior Finance Business Partner, Finance Shared Services, FP&A and Controller for global markets. You will work closely with the entire FBP finance team to share insights, recommendations, and reports to support decision making.

Additional focus is on supporting the businesses on executing their strategic operational objectives and undertaking ad hoc analyses and special projects.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES INCLUDE:

Leadership Support

Serve as connection point between internal Business Operations leaders (VPs and Directors), leadership support teams in the following areas:

–       Capital Delivery

–       Services

–       Investment

–       Controllership (P2P Strategic Initiatives)

–       Corporate FP&A

–       IT Services

  • Provide meaningful, actionable insights, advice, and recommendations on historic and the future financial performance to the Business’ leadership
  • Support the business in protecting, growing and optimizing the assets of the Firm
  • Support initiatives to help the business in achieving its strategic objectives

Monitoring of Period Performance and Execution of Financial/Ops Routines

Explain, present and analyze the financial performance of the business. With a primary focus on engagement (managed) views of financials and profitability drivers as well as understanding demand trends

  • Focus on transforming information into insights and actionable decisions
  • Support the management of financial risks and the implementation & maintenance of controls
  • Collaborate with the Finance Lead, COO, and CoS to prepare analyses related to sales, pipeline, revenue, profitability, operational performance and other KPIs
  • Understand firm systems and data to assess implications of operational changes on reporting
  • Innovate next generation of leadership decision-making support tools and routines
  • Support the delivery of the relevant business area’s financial response to price controls and maximize future allowances for ongoing investment in infrastructure and services
  • Continuously improve Consulting Finance and BP&A’s organizational efficiency and effectiveness by leading internal change initiatives

Forecast, Planning, and Ad Hoc Support

  • Support forecasting and planning processes as needed so that key inputs are considered
  • Partner with peers within and across the other Offering Portfolios to share ideas and collaborate on ways to improve finance routines and processes
  • Conduct other financial analysis and reporting as needed
  • Present the monthly results in a style suitable for both a finance and non-finance audience, delivering insight and highlighting priority areas for focus, ensuring efficiencies are optimized for the longer term. Provide action plans to address issues and ensure corrective actions are delivered on a timely basis.

ABOUT THE TEAM:

Directly supporting VP / Director level Business stakeholders

QUALIFICATIONS:

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance or related area
  • CPA / MBA / ACA / ACCA / CIMA or evidence of similar qualified experience preferred

Technology or Other Requirements

  • Strong understanding of regulated business within a global firm
  • Apptio Software and other financial applications desired
  • Expert in Excel, advanced in PowerPoint and Word
  • Understanding of Tableau capabilities for data visualization

Experience Requirements

Minimum of 4+ years of relevant finance experience, preferably in a professional services firm and/or with a focus on Financial Reporting & Analysis

Demonstrated accomplishments in the following areas:

  • Financial Reporting and Analysis
  • Quality deliverable development
  • Communication skills with all levels (from leadership to staff)
  • Working in virtual teams and coordinating across other groups
  • Problem solving skills
  • Quality and risk management
  • Change management
  • Business process improvement
  • Formulating and delivering action plans for high profile strategic initiatives
  • Developing innovative solutions to business issues 

CAPABILITY AND SKILLSET REQUIREMENTS:

  • Accountability for Results: Holds self and others accountable for ensuring that results are achieved; Seizes opportunities to produce results without direct supervision; Takes action on items beyond formal job responsibility; Sets high expectations for self and others
  • Communications: Strong communication skills, excellent interpersonal skills, both oral and written, and ability to create as well as deliver effective presentations
  • Creating Quality Deliverable: Establishes deliverable structure and content; Reviews deliverables of self and others team to ensure that they meet client expectations; Implements and enforces high standards for quality deliverables
  • Developing thought leadership / innovative ideas: Generates innovative ideas that are sound and progressive; Challenges the status quo; Fosters creativity throughout area of responsibility
  • Problem Solving: Ability to work in a dynamic environment that requires critical thinking and strong analytical skills
  • Providing Exceptional Client Service: Manages self and others to provide exceptional client service by responding with a sense of urgency, practicality, accountability, and integrity
  • Prioritization: Able to manage multiple projects or initiatives concurrently, in a productive manner
  • Building Rapport: Leads by example; actively listens; Respects the opinions and perspectives of others; Demonstrates strong personal commitment to others; clearly states thoughts, ideas, and expectations
  • Decision-making: Decision making skills, and ability to challenge, negotiate, and influence
  • Accounting: Understanding GAAP and firm accounting policies and procedure

title: Senior IT Risk & Compliance Specialist
description:
Job Overview

Devonshire Recruitment & Consulting Partners is seeking a Senior IT Risk & Compliance Specialist to lead governance, risk, and compliance initiatives across the organization. This role serves as a functional IT Security Lead, working with business managers to implement ethical business practices, ensure regulatory compliance, and develop enterprise-wide governance programs.

Key Responsibilities

  • Develop and maintain governance policies aligned with industry standards and frameworks (NIST, COSO, COBIT, etc.).
  • Communicate, implement, and monitor policies across departments to ensure compliance.
  • Conduct application gap assessments, establish security requirements, and perform risk assessments.
  • Provide risk management consulting services to internal teams, helping prioritize remediation efforts.
  • Oversee the General Computer Control (GCC) environment, identifying risks and implementing mitigating controls.
  • Facilitate operational-level risk assessments and act as a bridge to enterprise-level risk management.
  • Support both Agile and Waterfall project methodologies while managing multiple responsibilities within the IT risk function.

Qualifications

  • 8+ years in IT Audit, Governance, Risk & Compliance, Internal Audit, or related roles.
  • Proven experience in policy creation, implementation, monitoring, and maintenance.
  • Relevant certification (CISA, CISM, CISSP, CRISC, CRMA) or eligibility.
  • Skilled in developing and implementing controls and processes via recognized frameworks.
  • Strong background in IT risk assessment, controls, and project management.
  • Excellent communication, relationship-building, and problem-solving skills.
  • Ability to manage sensitive issues professionally and proactively.

title: Principal .NET Engineer
description:
Devonshire Recruiting & Consulting Partners is actively seeking a skilled and experienced Principal .NET Engineer who can drive impactful software experiences and deliver them utilizing the latest backend technologies and modern engineering best practices. You will collaborate with a talented team of engineers, designers, and project managers throughout the full software development lifecycle. This role is perfect for someone who understands the value of strong relationships and strives to consistently exceed client expectations by delivering high-quality, scalable solutions.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Develop clean, efficient, and scalable code using .NET programming languages.
  • Stay informed on current terminology, best practices, and advancements in coding.
  • Design, develop, and maintain technical interfaces, system architecture, and web applications.
  • Collaborate with teams to adapt and improve existing applications.
  • Create and test software prototypes and applications.
  • Design and implement user interfaces and client-facing displays.
  • Assist with project requirements, coordinating with team members and other developers to ensure smooth project execution.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent experience in training or fellowships.
  • 7-10+ years of hands-on experience in developing large-scale enterprise applications using .NET/.NET Core in an Agile or Scrum environment.
  • Strong expertise in microservices architecture, web technologies, and enterprise applications.
  • Solid foundational knowledge of JavaScript and experience working with Git.
  • Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic startup environment.
  • Familiarity with other open-source languages and both object-oriented and functional programming paradigms.
  • Willingness to quickly adapt to and learn new technologies and tools to solve problems effectively.
  • Proficient in modern browser capabilities and technologies.
  • Knowledge of software design patterns and a demonstrated ability to debug and manage complex systems.
  • Awareness of user experience (UX) design principles and attention to detail.
  • Excellent communication skills, driven by a positive attitude, empathy, and a desire for continuous improvement.

Desired Skills:

  • Proficiency with the ASP.NET framework, SQL Server, Entity Framework, MVC, C#, and .NET.
  • Familiarity with REST and RPC architecture styles/APIs.
  • Strong knowledge of Agile methodologies.
  • Excellent problem-solving and troubleshooting abilities.
  • Ability to work effectively in a collaborative team environment.

Bonus Skills:

  • Experience with React.js is preferred 
  • Expertise in benchmarking and optimizing JavaScript performance.
  • Experience designing scalable applications and systems.
  • Familiarity with backend technologies, including Node and .NET.
  • Experience migrating legacy applications to modern frameworks and architectures.

title: Senior Security Consultant
location: Hybrid (Cambridge, MA, Los Angeles, CA, Philadelphia, PA, or Virginia)
description:
Job Overview

Devonshire Recruiting & Consulting Partners is seeking a Senior Security Consultant to join a leading multi-disciplinary design consulting firm. This hybrid role, offering a flexible schedule, can be based in Cambridge, MA, Los Angeles, CA, Philadelphia, PA, or Virginia. The ideal candidate is a proactive, client-focused professional with expertise in ICT and physical security system design, strong communication skills, and a passion for delivering innovative solutions in a collaborative, dynamic environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design and document ICT and physical security systems, ensuring high-quality deliverables for diverse projects.
  • Manage projects from conception to completion, coordinating with clients, stakeholders, and internal teams.
  • Collaborate with CAD staff to produce accurate project documentation and contribute to the firm’s drafting standards.
  • Engage in client-facing activities, providing technical expertise and fostering strong relationships.
  • Support business development efforts, including attending networking events, delivering presentations at non-peer conferences, and authoring content for business-oriented publications.
  • Leverage proficiency in Revit and Bluebeam to enhance project delivery and documentation.
  • Work independently and in teams, managing multiple projects simultaneously with a proactive approach.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field (e.g., engineering, security systems, or related discipline).
  • 5-7 years of experience in ICT and physical security system design, consulting, and project management.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, with proven client-facing experience.
  • Proficiency in Revit and Bluebeam software.
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively on multiple projects in a fast-paced environment.
  • BICSI RCDD certification preferred; ASIS PSP or CPTED certifications are a plus.
  • Proactive, self-motivated mindset with a commitment to delivering high-quality results.
  • Strong interpersonal skills and a dedication to fostering an inclusive, team-oriented culture.

title: Senior Software Engineer (.NET Framework / Cloud)
location: North of Boston, MA (Salem, Beverly, Danvers area)
description:
Job Overview

Devonshire Recruiting & Consulting Partners is actively seeking a Senior Software Developer with deep expertise in the Microsoft .NET Framework (4.8), C#, and Windows-based desktop application development. This position is ideal for experienced engineers who thrive in data-heavy environments and are passionate about writing efficient, maintainable code with a focus on clean architecture, robust performance, and graphical precision. The role may be performed remotely (U.S.-based) or from a location near Boston’s North Shore.

This is an excellent opportunity for an engineer who prefers mastering a proven technology stack, enjoys working on long-lifecycle products, and wants to play a critical role in the ongoing cloud optimization and long-term architectural transition of a legacy application.

Key Responsibilities
  • Maintain and refine a production-grade WinForms application built on .NET Framework 4.8.
  • Enhance system performance, reliability, and scalability in coordination with AWS-hosted SQL environments and supporting cloud services.
  • Ensure secure data handling aligned with GDPR standards and implement modern authentication frameworks (e.g., OAuth2, SAML).
  • Contribute to the design and rollout of a strategic migration from a legacy desktop platform to a future web-based solution.
  • Support the design of a CI/CD pipeline to increase deployment efficiency and testing rigor.
  • Collaborate on experimental product enhancements and A/B testing initiatives to inform UX simplification.
  • Identify and implement modular code patterns for functionality reuse across multiple user personas.
  • Collaborate with stakeholders and subject matter experts to support the development of complex data-driven and mathematical features.
  • Utilize AI-enhanced tooling where applicable to accelerate quality software delivery.
Required Qualifications
  • Minimum of 5 years’ professional experience in software engineering with a focus on C# and .NET Framework development.
  • Strong hands-on experience developing WinForms applications in Visual Studio.
  • Proficient in SQL Server performance tuning and schema design, particularly in AWS-based deployments.
  • Familiarity with compliance standards (GDPR) and security best practices in software development environments.
  • Experience integrating modern authentication mechanisms such as OAuth2, OpenID Connect, and SAML.
  • Skilled in designing scalable and maintainable software systems with long-term roadmaps and migration considerations.
  • Exposure to CI/CD practices; experience with tools such as Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions, or similar is preferred.
  • Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate complex data sets into clear graphical output.
  • Comfortable working in an iterative development environment with quarterly releases and regular patching cycles.
  • Effective communicator with the ability to interface with technical leads, product owners, and business users.

Experience Required: Mid-Career – Senior

title: Senior Vulnerability Analyst
location: Westbrook, ME (preferred) or Remote within EST/CST
description:
Job Overview

Devonshire Recruitment & Consulting Partners is seeking a Senior Vulnerability Management / Security Analyst to join a growing Information Security team. This role combines hands-on technical execution with strategic program development, advancing the organization’s vulnerability management maturity across on-premise and multi-cloud environments.

Key Responsibilities

  • Perform vulnerability assessments across infrastructure, cloud, containers, and applications, correlating findings with threat intelligence for risk-based prioritization.
  • Collaborate with teams to remediate vulnerabilities, track progress, and enforce consistent patching.
  • Build and scale a structured vulnerability lifecycle program, including the automation of recurring workflows and the integration of security tools (e.g., GHAS, Aqua) into pipelines.
  • Monitor emerging vulnerabilities and support incident response as needed.
  • Produce reports and dashboards that reflect the risk posture and program progress.
  • Mentor junior staff, provide operational coverage, and drive continuous improvement of vulnerability management practices.

Required Qualifications

  • 8–10 years in vulnerability management, security engineering, or related cybersecurity roles.
  • Strong expertise in vulnerability scanning, cloud/container security, code analysis, and remediation workflows.
  • Experience building or maturing enterprise vulnerability management programs.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Cybersecurity, Computer Science, or related field (or equivalent experience).
  • Proficiency with scripting/automation (Python, Bash, PowerShell) preferred.
  • Relevant certifications (CISSP, OSCP, GCIH, or similar) are a plus.
  • Strong analytical, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
  • Adaptable and proactive, capable of balancing tactical execution with strategic program growth.

title: Software Engineer (Full-Stack)
description:
Job Overview

Devonshire Recruitment & Consulting Partners is seeking a Full-Stack Software Engineer to join a dynamic, evolving team in the medical industry. You’ll help build and scale a seamless consumer healthcare platform, working across the stack to deliver impactful solutions. This is a hands-on role for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design, build, and maintain web applications that power the healthcare marketplace.
  • Collaborate with product, design, operations, and clinical teams to deliver patient-first solutions.
  • Write requirements, test features, and analyze performance with SQL and other tools.
  • Manage technical tradeoffs between speed and long-term quality.
  • Contribute to healthcare tech integrations (EHRs, revenue cycle, etc.) and innovative initiatives like LLMs and price transparency.

Required Qualifications

  • 2+ years of professional full-stack engineering experience.
  • Proficiency in a modern stack; AWS, Ruby on Rails, and Next.js preferred.
  • Strong problem-solving, collaboration, and communication skills.
  • Comfortable working in ambiguous, high-growth environments with cross-functional teams.
  • Prior healthcare experience is a plus, but not required.

Compensation & Benefits

  • Salary: $150,000 – $180,000 + equity.
  • Schedule: Hybrid, 3 days/week.
  • Benefits: Insurance coverage (UnitedHealthcare), unlimited PTO, 401(k), and career growth opportunities with a mission-driven, well-funded startup.

title: Software Engineer – Python
description:
Devonshire Recruiting & Consulting Partners is currently seeking a full time Software Engineer to support the new development and customization of our client’s product suite. This role requires full life cycle development including, but not limited to gathering client requirements, designing solutions, coding and integration, testing and post-production support.

Qualifications:

  • 2+ years of full time coding, customization and integration using Python and APIs
  • Web technologies experience such as HTML5, CSS3 and JavaScript (AngularJS)
  • Strong back-end coding experience including SQL query development
  • Knowledge working in Linux environments and command line
  • Undderstanding of networking protocols (TCP/IP)
  • Strong understanding of HTTP, RESTful APIs and SOAP
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills as this role does require client interaction
  • This is a client interfacing role
  • BS Degree in Computer Science or equivalent

title: Sr. Infrastructure Support Administrator
description:
Job Overview

Devonshire Recruiting & Consulting Partners is actively seeking a skilled and detail-oriented Sr. Infrastructure Support Administrator to join a dynamic team based in Lowell, MA. This full-time role offers the opportunity to take ownership of the day-to-day administration, security, and optimization of network infrastructure while ensuring a high-performance and reliable IT environment. This position is integral to supporting infrastructure improvements, troubleshooting, and maintaining system integrity across various environments.

Key Responsibilities

  • Manage and oversee hardware, firmware, and operating systems for physical and virtual technology environments, including servers, routers, switches, firewalls, VPN, Active Directory, VMware, and network monitoring.
  • Manage and maintain a secure, high-performing multi-site Windows Active Directory environment, including Group Policies and Windows Server Operating Systems.
  • Actively engage in implementing infrastructure improvements by developing project plans, leading team activities, maintaining upgrade schedules, and recommending enhancements.
  • Monitor and troubleshoot network performance issues, proposing solutions to improve efficiency, availability, and overall system performance.
  • Ensure network integrity and security by implementing and managing access controls, patch management, vulnerability remediation, and firewall security.
  • Supervise the Infrastructure Support help desk to ensure prompt and effective resolution of user requests and manage escalated matters as necessary.
  • Collaborate with external service providers to ensure contract compliance and reliable service delivery.
  • Design, document, and update business continuity plans, as well as create and maintain network diagrams and procedural documentation.
  • Train, mentor, and guide department employees while overseeing daily operations within the team.
  • Play a leadership role in department initiatives and strategic projects, focusing on infrastructure enhancements and technological advancements.
  • Participate in on-call rotation for off-hours critical systems monitoring and support, carrying a cell phone to respond promptly to system issues and emergencies.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in a computer technical field or a related discipline, or an equivalent combination of education and experience.
  • 3-5 years of experience in infrastructure support or a similar role.
  • Proficiency in Windows Operating Systems, Microsoft Active Directory, and virtual environments.
  • Knowledge of network security protocols, including patch management, access controls, and vulnerability remediation.
  • Familiarity with Network+ certification or VMware Certified Professional designation (desired).
  • Experience with financial institutions or credit unions (preferred).
  • Strong communication skills with the ability to work cohesively with cross-functional teams.
  • Willingness to work extended hours and participate in on-call rotations to support critical system issues and initiatives.
  • Proven ability to document processes, create network diagrams, and maintain up-to-date technical documentation.

title: Systems Administration Engineer
description:
Devonshire Recruitment & Consulting Partners is seeking a skilled Systems Administration Engineer to join the Technical Services team for a leading financial organization. In this role, you will manage, maintain, and enhance the organization’s IT infrastructure, ensuring the availability, stability, and security of systems that support daily operations. The ideal candidate will possess strong technical expertise, experience in systems administration, and a proactive approach to problem-solving and process improvement.

Key Responsibilities

  • Manage and support infrastructure platforms, including Dell/HP servers, VMware (ESX, VRA, VDI), Rubrik, and Microsoft Active Directory/Exchange.
  • Oversee patching, backups, monitoring, and auditing systems to maintain platform health and security.
  • Provide day-to-day management and level 2 support for infrastructure and user platforms in collaboration with internal teams.
  • Handle data center hardware and vendor support tickets.
  • Lead team projects and user requests through Jira and Jira Service Desk, ensuring timely resolution and tracking.
  • Develop and enforce IT standards, policies, and best practices, focusing on security compliance and remediation.
  • Assist the Service Desk team with desktop, VDI, phone, and mobile device support as needed.
  • Respond to service desk requests promptly via chat, phone, email, or in person.
  • Support employee mobile devices through Mobile Device Management (MDM) platforms.
  • Enhance documentation for troubleshooting and resolution procedures.
  • Participate in evening and weekend work for changes, patching, or critical updates as required.

Required Qualifications

  • 7+ years of experience in a technical support role within IT or development.
  • Hands-on experience with multiple industry-standard technology platforms, including:
    • Dell/HP/Cisco Servers, EMC hardware
    • Microsoft Windows and Exchange
    • VMware and VMware Horizon
  • Strong problem-solving skills and proven expertise in resolving complex issues across applications.
  • Effective communication and interpersonal skills to collaborate across teams and build trust through strong job performance.
  • Experience as a technical lead, providing guidance and fostering professional growth among team members.
  • Proficiency in managing documentation, troubleshooting, and implementation processes.

This opportunity offers a collaborative, team-oriented environment where professionalism, a positive attitude, and initiative are highly valued.

title: Technical Group Director
description:
Job Overview

Devonshire Recruiting & Consulting Partners is actively seeking a Technical Group Director on behalf of our client, a nationally recognized leader in acoustics, technology systems, and security consulting. This position will lead the Technology Group, overseeing the delivery of advanced low-voltage design and engineering solutions, including AV, ICT, and Security services. Based out of Cambridge, MA, the Director will report directly to the President and be a key member of the executive leadership team.

This high-visibility role is ideal for an accomplished leader with strong technical management experience, excellent interpersonal communication, and a passion for guiding a seasoned consulting team. The ideal candidate will bring a strategic mindset, exceptional client engagement skills, and a collaborative leadership style.

Key Responsibilities

  • Develop and communicate a forward-looking strategic plan for the Technology Group, aligned with the company’s overall goals.
  • Define and promote the group’s market positioning and technical value proposition.
  • Guide group structure, resource planning, and workflows to support project delivery and operational efficiency.
  • Direct business development strategy and lead client acquisition, proposal efforts, and industry engagement activities.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with Marketing, Finance, HR, and other technical teams to support company-wide objectives.
  • Foster a culture of innovation, integrity, teamwork, and professional excellence across the group.
  • Lead budgeting efforts, set utilization targets, and monitor group performance metrics.
  • Represent the Technology Group at quarterly board meetings and contribute to corporate strategy discussions.
  • Manage the group’s project pipeline, overseeing resource allocation and supporting project execution where needed.
  • Mentor, evaluate, and develop staff across the department, including conducting performance reviews and planning compensation.
  • Oversee coordination with HR on recruitment, onboarding, retention, and employee relations.
  • Collaborate with accounting on financial oversight, project billing, and contracts.
  • Actively participate in marketing and BD efforts, including RFP responses, conference presentations, and industry events.
  • Champion equity, diversity, inclusion, and belonging (EDIB) initiatives in hiring and team culture.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in engineering, technology, systems design, or a related field required; advanced degree preferred.
  • Minimum 10 years of leadership experience in consulting, technology systems design, or low-voltage disciplines such as AV, telecom, or security.
  • Demonstrated success in group-level strategy, business development, and cross-functional collaboration.
  • Exceptional communication and leadership skills; ability to guide technical teams and engage with executive stakeholders.
  • Proven track record managing project teams, budgets, and departmental KPIs.
  • Experience with industry associations, conferences, or presenting technical topics publicly is a plus.

title: Technical Implementation & Operations Manager
description:
Job Overview

Devonshire Recruiting & Consulting Partners is actively seeking a dynamic and technically proficient Technical Implementation & Operations Manager to support electricity-focused software integrations for a rapidly growing clean energy technology employer. This individual will be responsible for managing the full lifecycle of metering infrastructure software deployments, coordinating across internal teams and external partners to ensure operational success from initial pilot through long-term sustainment.

This is a high-visibility role requiring a balance of technical depth, operational oversight, and exceptional stakeholder management capabilities.

Key Responsibilities
  • Oversee the end-to-end implementation and operational health of smart meter deployments, working across engineering, product, infrastructure, customer support, and utility program teams.
  • Act as the primary liaison between the company and external meter manufacturers, ensuring technical alignment and execution against deployment timelines.
  • Develop detailed project plans and milestones that align with customer schedules; track progress and adjust resources as needed to stay on course.
  • Drive cross-functional communication across internal departments and external stakeholders to ensure smooth execution and consistent service delivery.
  • Identify deployment risks or technical roadblocks early and proactively mitigate them through structured problem-solving and stakeholder engagement.
  • Lead the transition from implementation to long-term support by establishing scalable workflows and performance benchmarks.
  • Design and maintain monitoring protocols and key metrics (KPIs) to track system health, reliability, and network connectivity post-deployment.
